rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN WONG's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD GRANTED. Counsel prepare order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MAGCO DRILLING, INC. VS. NATOMA FAMILY HOUSING, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 3. DEFENDANT NATOMA FAMILY HOUSING, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, ST. JOSEPH'S FAMILY ASSOCIATES, L.P.'s, AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. The only cause of action alleged in the complaint is for Foreclosure of Mechanics Lien. Moving party shifted its burned and presented competent admissible evidence that the Lien has not been timely recorded by CC 8414(b)(1) as the work has been complete within the meaning of CC 8180 as of February 1, 2014. Defendant fails to present competent admissible evidence to the contrary; and no authority is presented for the proposition that the date of the Notice (March 17, 2014 or May 14, 2014) is controlling or prompt payment provisions are applicable. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EVERETT ADAMS ET AL VS. DIPAK PATEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Made Pursuant To Ccp Section 473 B For Relief From Order Granting Defendant Tenderloin Housing Clinic Incs Motion To Compel Plainitffs To Respond To Requests For Admissions Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Document Requests And Request Fro Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Coundel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 5. PLAINTIFF EVERETT ADAMS, DELORIS BROWN, RUSSELL CAIN, DARYL DANIELS, EDWARD DAVIS' Notice Of Motion Made Pursuant To Ccp Section 473 B For Relief From Order Granting Defendant Tenderloin Housing Clinic Incs Motion To Compel Plainitffs To Respond To Requests For Admissions Form Interrogatories Special Interrogatories And Document Requests And Request Fro Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiffs And Their Coundel Of Record Hearing required to address: (1) the delay (October 20/November 3 to November 15) in filing this motion; (2) failure to comply with the tentative ruling rule re: October 20, 2016 hearing; (3) failure to provide courtesy copy of the moving papers (counsel was ordered to comply with local rules on two prior occasions in this action). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANA VANDERLEY VS. JAMES D KELLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Authorization For Release Of Medical Records
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 6. rulings.Case Title: WENDY HENRIQUEZ VS. SALLY LIU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 7. CROSS DEFENDANT WENDY HENRIQUEZ's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend for the cross-complaint to allege facts in support of each cause of action. Cross-complainant may assert additional causes of action. The Court notes that cross-complainant failed to comply with CCP 430.41 meet and confer provisions. Cross-complainant is ordered to comply in future. Failure to comply may result in a OSC hearing. The Court notes that cross-defendant failed to provide a courtesy copy of the challenged cross-complaint in violation of SFLR 2.7B. Failure to comply in future may result in demurrer being overruled or taken off calendar. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAMANTHA LEE SETO MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. MARTHE SCHREIBER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 05, 2016 line 8. DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff "71 Water Partners" to clarify whether it is an owner, authorized property manager or other. The Court notes that neither party complied with this Court's October 26. 2016 order. Plaintiff was granted leave to substitute the Plaintiff by the owner of the property. The Amendment filed on October 26, 2016 failed to identify the substituted Plaintiff "71 Water Partners" as an owner. Defendant was ordered to file an answer. Defendant did not contest the tentative ruling and did not seek leave of Court prior to filing of the present demurrer. = (501/REQ)
